latest.title: Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Trading Services
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1673Y
GSTechnologies Ltd
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Asset Trading Services
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces the temporary suspension of the Company's crypto asset trading services with effect from 15 April 2026.
On 31 December 2025 the Company announced that GS Fintech UAB's crypto asset trading platform had transitioned to operate under the VASP-registered entity Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno") in Poland.
Whilst this can continue until 30 June 2026 the Company has determined that the Company's crypto asset trading activities should be aligned with the requirements of the EU Regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets ("MiCA") as soon as possible.
Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026, Finferno will temporarily suspend all services relating to the trading of crypto assets. The suspension will remain in place until a MiCA licence is obtained. Finferno's Terms of Service will be updated in connection with the suspension and will take effect on the same date.
Users of the platform are being informed and further announcements will be made in due course, as appropriate.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8371S
GSTechnologies Ltd
13 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
13 February 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces an update regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
As announced on 18 July 2025, the Company issued a notice of arbitration to Choo Seet EE and Zheng Kang Wen Mervyn (the "Sellers"), the sellers of Semnet, as set out in the Sale and Purchase Agreement ("SPA") dated 5 December 2023. As referenced in the Company's interim results announced on 18 December 2025, a mediation hearing was held in early December 2025 with a view to agreeing a formal settlement agreement between the Sellers and the Company. However, formal settlement terms have not been reached, and consequently, Semnet has now formally issued and served a writ of summons against the Sellers and Hoi Yan ('Daphane') Chong (a former Semnet manager) to vigorously pursue claims for alleged breaches of fiduciary and contractual duties, including breaches of the SPA, that have negatively impacted the Semnet business. The total damages sought are approximately US$4.2 million.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Completion of Metapay Acquisition
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0506Q
GSTechnologies Ltd
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Completion of Metapay Acquisition
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce the successful completion of its acquisition of Metapay SP. z o.o. ("Metapay").
Further to the Company's interim results announcement on 18 December 2025, GST confirms that it has now completed the acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of Metapay, a company incorporated in Poland.
Metapay holds a Small Payment Institution (SPI) licence and is registered under the Polish Act on Payment Services with MIP260/2025 status. The acquisition is aligned with GST's strategy to enhance its footprint in domestic and cross-border payment services across Europe.
Following completion, the Company has renamed Metapay to Angra Limited sp. z o.o. and, together with the anticipated progress of its licensing initiatives, this is expected to support the continued expansion of Angra Global's service offerings and geographical reach.

latest.title: End of MiCA Transitional Period in Lithuania
latest.content:
31 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1900N
GSTechnologies Ltd
31 December 2025
31 December
2025
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
End of MiCA Transitional Period in Lithuania
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces that
following the submission of a
MiCA license application to the Bank of Lithuania in September 2025,
GS Fintech UAB has not obtained crypto-asset service provider (CASP) authorisation under MiCA by the end of the transitional period, which expires on 31 December 2025.
The Company plans to resubmit its application to the Bank of Lithuania in Q1 2026 and in the meantime, to ensure trading continuity without interruption, GS Fintech UAB's platform will transition to operations under VASP-registered Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością in Poland, whose acquisition by the Company was announced on 29 December 2025.

latest.title: Acquisition of Finferno
latest.content:
29 Dec 2025 10:08
RNS Number : 9872M
GSTechnologies Ltd
29 December 2025
29 December
2025
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Acquisition of Finferno
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce that the Company has entered into a legally binding agreement to acquire the entire issued share capital of Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno"), a Polish-registered Virtual Asset Service Provider ("VASP"), for an undisclosed cash consideration (the "Acquisition").
The Acquisition, which is being funded through the Company's existing cash resources, is intended to facilitate the growth of the Group's digital asset business, focused on both digital asset exchange and wealth management, in Poland and Central Europe. In particular, the Acquisition will enable the Group to launch new offerings in Poland, initially on a pilot basis. GST views Poland as a particularly attractive area for future growth given the country's robust economic growth forecasts and surging cryptocurrency adoption.
Tone Goh, Chairman of GST, commented:
"I am very pleased to be announcing the acquisition of Finferno, which will add to GST's growing digital asset capabilities. T
his acquisition is in line with our plans to grow our digital asset business,
GS Fintech,
internationally in selected markets where we see strong potential. Poland, and Central Europe more widely, offer attractive opportunities for GST, and we are keen to invest more in the region, which is enjoying significant economic growth and burgeoning cryptocurrency adoption. As we enter 2026 GST is very well positioned and I look forward to providing further updates in due course.
"

company_30s.what_they_do: GST Ltd. builds and sells crypto‑wallet and payment solutions for consumers and businesses, focusing on secure, low‑fee transactions across multiple blockchains.
company_30s.why_they_matter: With the global shift toward digital assets, GST’s wallet platform is one of the few that integrates mainstream banking with crypto, giving it a foothold in a market that’s still expanding into everyday payments.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a survival mode: it has paused crypto trading, is restructuring governance, and is trying to turn the recent Metapay acquisition into a revenue stream. It’s still trading at a fraction of its historic highs.

rns_narrative: GST is juggling regulatory hurdles and a strategic pivot. The temporary halt of crypto trading and a delay in MiCA compliance show the company is still ironing out operational issues, but the Metapay buy‑out and new Bitcoin treasury policy signal a clear move toward monetisation.

Three themes emerge: (1) Regulatory turbulence – suspensions, auditor changes, and MiCA deadlines; (2) Strategic realignment – Metapay integration, Bitcoin holdings, and a new semnet platform; (3) Governance shake‑ups – director share purchases, arbitration notices, and delayed accounts.
The company seems to be stabilising after the acquisition, but it’s still waiting for regulatory green lights and a clear path to consistent earnings.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet watchfulness – no loud voices, just a handful of cautious optimists.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the Metapay deal and the Bitcoin treasury as evidence that GST is finally finding a profitable niche.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others flag the suspension of crypto services, repeated governance delays, and the lack of fresh earnings data as signs that the company could stay mired in cash‑burn mode.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially neutral chatter turned slightly bullish after the Metapay announcement, but the silence that followed keeps the overall mood low‑key.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no current public buzz, which means a surprise catalyst could hit the market with maximum impact.

near_term_catalysts: The resumption of crypto trading services, if announced by the next earnings release, would likely lift volume and price.
A clear revenue contribution from the Metapay integration – for example, a 20‑30% YoY lift in monthly active users – would give the market confidence.
Positive Q4 earnings that beat consensus by at least 10% would validate the new business model.
MiCA compliance approval or a clear regulatory roadmap would remove a major uncertainty.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that is at least 3 times the 30‑day average, a break above the 20‑day moving average, and any earnings beat or product‑launch announcement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a deep crash. If the catalysts above materialise, a mean‑reversion bounce could begin. Until then, the price remains highly volatile.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: GST is down 86% of its peak – that’s a 4‑to‑1 upside if it rebounds to the midpoint between current and historic highs. The Metapay acquisition and Bitcoin policy suggest a new revenue engine that could drive a breakout. Historically, deep‑crash setups that get a catalyst deliver the biggest returns.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory delays, the ongoing suspension of crypto services, and a history of governance hiccups could push the price lower. If the company fails to turn the Metapay integration into cash, the next drop could be another 20‑30%. The worst‑case is a further 10‑15% slide before the market deems the stock unsalvageable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 86% of the current price if the bounce fails. Potential gain: about 300% if the price climbs to the halfway mark between current and peak. Risk‑reward is roughly 4:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to a key support level (roughly 5% above the recent low). Allocate 10‑15% of the portfolio to this position. Set a stop 10% below support to limit loss to about 15% of the entry price.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger an order when crypto trading resumes or when Q4 earnings beat expectations by 10%+ and volume spikes beyond 3× the 30‑day average.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the company has no imminent bankruptcy or delisting risk.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GST is a deep‑crashed play with a clear catalyst path through Metapay integration and crypto resumption; the upside is large, the downside is capped, making it a contrarian bullish bet.
